title: Pork belly – with herbs and lemon
excerpt: Just look how a lovely piece of pork belly tastes when its time comes. I'm sharing the recipe because it's something easy for your Christmas table. It can be enjoyed cold or warm. Instead of roast potatoes I made lovely roast sweet potatoes. The recipe doesn't really have exact quantities of ingredients …

seo.title: Pork belly - with herbs and lemon - Marinda Kook

html:
Just look how a lovely piece of pork belly tastes when its time comes. I'm sharing the recipe because it's something easy for your Christmas table. It can be enjoyed cold or warm. Instead of roast potatoes I made lovely roast sweet potatoes. The recipe doesn't really have exact quantities of ingredients. It's more about the combination of the ingredients.

 

YOU NEED:

  • 1 pork belly
  • 4 cloves of garlic
  • 1 onion sliced into rings
  • a few sprigs of thyme
  • 100 ml water
  • 1 lemon's zest finely grated and the juice of the lemon
  • 100 ml finely chopped rosemary
  • 100 ml finely chopped parsley
  • coarse salt
  • coarse black pepper
  • chilli flakes or finely chopped chilli
  • 90 ml olive oil
  • 4 sweet potatoes peeled

NOW:

  1. set your oven to 180 degrees
  2. chop the parsley, rosemary and lemon zest finely
  3. mix it with the salt, pepper, olive oil, lemon juice and chilli flakes
  4. rub the pork belly nicely with the mixture
  5. put the onion, garlic, thyme and water in the bottom of your roasting dish
  6. place your pork belly on top of the bed of onions
  7. bake it now uncovered in the oven for 90 minutes
  8. cut your sweet potatoes into quarters and place them back in the oven together with the pork belly, turn the sweet potatoes from time to time – leave it in the oven for another 45 minutes or so until the sweet potatoes are brown and cooked through.
  9. the crackling should be nice and crisp, if it isn't crisp, grill it under the grill element of your stove

Enjoy the delicious piece of pork together with salad or vegetables. It can be served cold or warm. If there's anything left over, make yourself a lovely pork and mustard sandwich for work tomorrow.
